title: festuca curvula subsp. crassifolia
intro: Festuca curvula subsp. crassifolia, also known as Rough Fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family, native to South Africa. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its health benefits. The plant is used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and its extracts have been shown to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Traditionally, Rough Fescue is prepared as a tea or infusion for medicinal purposes, and its essential oils are used in aromatherapy for their calming and soothing effects.
